summary: S2 — declarative manifest + detect-only check_migrations CLI. Shipped docs/schema-current.json (declarative, advisory, off-hot-path: manifestVersion/currentSchemaVersion/minimumAiRouterVersion/doc-URLs + symbolic migrator IDs, NO shell strings) and ai_router/check_migrations.py (detect-only scanner; classifies clean/drift/ahead/unreadable vs LOCAL_SCHEMA_VERSION; terse/--verbose/--json; exit-non-zero-on-drift with --exit-zero; advisory --manifest-url fail-open+cached, --strict-manifest fail-loud; ASCII-only output for Windows). 21 new tests incl. the manifest==SESSION_STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION CI guard and the carried-risk-#2 v2-needs-chain test. Full suite: 988 passed, 1 skipped, 0 regressions. KEY DEVIATION (flagged): the carried-risk-#2 test empirically falsified the verdict's locked two-migrator bulk sequence — a genuine v2 file (explicit schemaVersion:2 + legacy triple) is skipped by BOTH lightweight-to-v4 and v3-to-v4; the v2->v3 step is a third existing migrator (migrate_session_state) the verdict omitted. Corrected bulk chain (no new migrator logic): migrate_session_state -> migrate_lightweight_to_canonical_v4 -> migrate_v3_to_v4. S2 invoked no router (self-verified via the test suite); $0 added to the $10 set NTE.

reason.specifics: S3 (pure-JS hot-path drift scan in claude-session-start-invoker.js + install path + the CI test pinning the JS constant to SESSION_STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION) builds directly on the S2 manifest/constant and the corrected bulk chain; same orchestrator keeps the drift-detection design and the verdict-deviation context warm.
